summary: British-Nigerian artist and member of the Young British Artists. Known for headless mannequin sculptures dressed in Dutch wax / Ankara fabric, including The Swing (After Fragonard) and Nelson's Ship in a Bottle. Uses an electric wheelchair due to left-side paralysis.

works.name: The Swing (After Fragonard)
works.description: Sculptural recreation of Fragonard's The Swing using a headless mannequin dressed in Dutch wax / Ankara fabric. Wikipedia

clues.clue: Headless mannequin recreation of Fragonard's painting The Swing, dressed in Dutch wax / Ankara fabric
examples: This artist's piece Nelson's Ship in a Bottle was commissioned for the Fourth Plinth of Trafalgar Square... known for his signature use of Dutch wax fabric, which he used for the outfit of a headless woman in a recreation of Fragonard's painting The Swing
a re-creation of a painting depicting 'happy accidents,' one of his many works to use headless mannequins and traditional Dutch wax fabric

works.name: Nelson's Ship in a Bottle (2010)
works.description: 1:30 scale replica of the HMS Victory inside a bottle, with sails made of patterned Ankara textiles symbolizing African identity and independence. Commissioned for the Fourth Plinth of Trafalgar Square. Wikipedia

images.caption: Nelson's Ship in a Bottle on the Fourth Plinth

clues.clue: 1:30 scale replica of the HMS Victory in a bottle; sails made of patterned textiles; placed on Fourth Plinth of Trafalgar Square
examples: This artist's works include a 1 to 30 replica of the HMS Victory
Shonibare depicted this ship 'in a Bottle' in an installation once held at Trafalgar Square. The sails of the ship consist of patterned textiles, used to symbolize African identity and independence
this British-Nigerian artist was not awarded the 2004 Turner Prize. This artist's piece Nelson's Ship in a Bottle was commissioned for the Fourth Plinth of Trafalgar Square

works.name: Diary of a Victorian Dandy
works.description: Series of five photographs in which Shonibare re-imagines scenes from Hogarth's A Rake's Progress, casting himself as a Black dandy in Victorian settings.

clues.clue: Series of photographs where the artist inserts himself as a 'black dandy' in Victorian settings, based on Hogarth's A Rake's Progress
examples: This artist lies in a bed, surrounded by maids, in a photograph which re-imagines a scene from The Rake's Progress

Shonibare has photographed himself as a 'black dandy' in the series Diary of a Victorian Dandy, which was based on a series of paintings that begins with this character being fitted for a new suit and ends with him in Bedlam

works.name: General / Biographical
works.description: British-Nigerian artist and member of the Young British Artists. Uses an electric wheelchair since he is paralyzed on his left side. Buys Dutch wax fabric at Brixton Market. Other works include Line Painting, The American Library, Medusa recreation, Mr. and Mrs. Andrews recreation, Gallantry and Criminal Conversation, and Hibiscus Rising.

clues.clue: Uses headless mannequins dressed in Dutch wax / Ankara fabric as signature style
examples: This Nigerian artist known for decapitating models from English painting and replacing their clothes with Dutch wax fabric in sculptures
This British-Nigerian artist and member of the Young British Artists uses many colorful Ankara cloths in his signature headless mannequin sculptures

clues.clue: Uses electric wheelchair; paralyzed on left side
examples: This artist uses an electric wheelchair since he is paralyzed on his left side
This artist's works Hibiscus Rising and Diary of a Victorian Dandy both make use of material that this artist usually buys at Brixton Market

clues.clue: Recreated Gainsborough's Mr. and Mrs. Andrews with headless mannequins and Dutch wax fabric
examples: In another use of headless mannequins and Dutch wax fabric, Shonibare recreated Mr. and Mrs. Andrews, a painting by this 18th-century artist

clues.clue: The American Library (2018): names of successful immigrants on spines of 3,200 books
examples: In 2018, this artist wrote the names of successful immigrants on the spines of 3,200 books as part of his installation The American Library

clues.clue: Nominated for 2004 Turner Prize (audience favorite but did not win)
examples: Despite being the audience favorite, this British-Nigerian artist was not awarded the 2004 Turner Prize

clues.clue: Recreated Caravaggio's Medusa with a Black model; snakes made of Ankara cloth
examples: Shonibare used a Black model in his recreation of a Caravaggio painting on a wooden shield depicting this woman. In Shonibare's work, the snakes that make up this woman's hair are made of Ankara cloth

clues.clue: 2023 sculpture Hibiscus Rising in Aire Park, tribute to victim of police violence, described as 'Our Angel of the North'
examples: This artist's 2023 sculpture of a flower in Aire Park, intended as a tribute to a victim of police violence, was described by locals as 'Our Angel of the North'

comprehensive_summary:
Yinka Shonibare is a British-Nigerian artist and member of the Young British Artists, most commonly identified by his signature use of headless mannequins dressed in colorful Dutch wax (Ankara) fabric. He is paralyzed on his left side and uses an electric wheelchair.

His most frequently clued works are The Swing (After Fragonard), a sculptural recreation of Fragonard's painting using a headless mannequin in Ankara fabric, and Nelson's Ship in a Bottle, a 1:30 scale replica of the HMS Victory with textile sails, commissioned for the Fourth Plinth of Trafalgar Square. The sails symbolize African identity and independence.

Diary of a Victorian Dandy is a series of photographs in which Shonibare inserts himself as a Black dandy in elaborate Victorian settings, based on Hogarth's A Rake's Progress. He buys his Dutch wax fabric at Brixton Market.

Other clued works include Line Painting (patterned circles with African textile patterns), The American Library (2018, names of successful immigrants on 3,200 book spines), a recreation of Gainsborough's Mr. and Mrs. Andrews, a recreation of Caravaggio's Medusa using a Black model with snakes made of Ankara cloth, Gallantry and Criminal Conversation (headless mannequins in period costumes), and a 2023 sculpture Hibiscus Rising in Aire Park described as 'Our Angel of the North.' He was the audience favorite for the 2004 Turner Prize but did not win.
